titleStmt.title: SII 15.81: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 81. (B.K. No. 79 of 1928-29.) GALGALI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a broken stone built into the wall of a house near the Parvatēśvara temple.

This is dated the Vīra-Vikrama year 26, Vṛisha śu. 1, Sunday, the month possibly being Pushya. Vīra-Vikrama year evidently stands for the Chālukya-Vikrama year. In the said cyclic year, śu. 1, Sunday occurred only in the month of Adhika Bhādrapada, though in Pausha, śu. 1, commencing on Sunday, ended on Monday. The details may be equated to 1101 A.D., July 28, taking the month to be Adhika Bhādrapada.
The epigraph seems to commemorate Kannaseṭṭi who died in a defensive fight against the aggressors. It mentions Tarddavāḍi-nāḍu and Niḍuvaṇe. The inscription is damaged and fragmentary.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
vīravikrama varṣada 26 neya viṣa saṁvatsarada pu
śuddha pāḍiva Ādivāradaṁdu tarddavāḍi nā
niḍuvaṇeya mēlāḷikeya
ṇḍiruḍeyurcuvāga kannaseṭṭi kādi
Digital edition of SII 15.81 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
